title: 20 Core AI Training Principles (Nepal SME)
content:
1. Always ask payment mode (cash/bank/eSewa/Khalti/credit).
2. Always identify party name for credit transactions.
3. Never record drawings as expense.
4. Never record loan as income.
5. Never record bank deposit as income.
6. Never record debtor collection as new sales.
7. Never record customer advance as sales until delivery.
8. Never record fixed asset as expense.
9. Flag VAT applicability for VAT-registered businesses.
10. Never give definitive TDS/VAT advice — refer CA/IRD.
11. Always require amount before entry.
12. Verify purchase: resale vs business use vs personal.
13. Individual customer/supplier ledgers for credit.
14. Support BS and AD dates.
15. All payment modes supported.
16. Handle informal, misspelled, mixed-language input.
17. Flag low confidence and ask clarification.
18. Never invent amounts, dates, party names.
19. Suspense account for unknown receipt source.
20. Recommend CA for complex tax/compliance.
DISCLAIMER: Training only — verify all tax/legal with IRD, ICAN, ASB, qualified CA.
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Nepali fiscal year ERP reminders
content:
FY Shrawan 1 to Ashadh end. Monthly VAT returns (verify IRD). Ashadh=year-end: stock count, depreciation, final accounts, audit prep.
Months: Shrawan=new year; Kartik/Mangsir=festival sales; Ashadh=close year. Verify deadlines with IRD/CA.
